chief_complaint: A 61-year-old man experienced cardiopulmonary arrest twice during treatment for diabetic ketoacidosis and hyperosmolar hyperglycemic syndrome.
history: The patient was treated for diabetic ketoacidosis and hyperosmolar hyperglycemic syndrome. He experienced cardiopulmonary arrest twice during treatment. Anemia progressed, and a fecal occult blood test was positive. Ischemic enteritis with multiple small ulcers in the cecum and deep ulcers in the ileum were observed with lower gastrointestinal endoscopy. Approximately 1 week after the examination, melena and intestinal obstruction developed. Fever and abdominal pain were intensified, and blood test findings markedly worsened.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Blood test findings worsened, and abdominal CT showed that the small intestine was dilated, the wall of the terminal ileum was thickened, and a small amount of ascites in the pelvis.
